rct_screen_evidence: "We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy)."
intervention: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily)
registered_endpoint: Overall survival (time from randomization to death from any cause)

1.1.quote: "Randomization was performed centrally by telephone with the use of a computerized algorithm, which was developed and maintained by the MRC Clinical Trials Unit at UCL. Minimization with a random element of 80% was used..." (Randomization and Masking)
1.1.justification: The description of a centrally administered computer‑generated algorithm with minimization indicates a truly random allocation sequence.

1.2.quote: "Randomization was performed centrally by telephone with the use of a computerized algorithm..." (Randomization and Masking)
1.2.justification: Central telephone randomisation ensures that the allocation could not be known or predicted by enrolling clinicians, satisfying allocation concealment.

1.3.quote: "Age at randomization – median (IQR) 67 (62 to 72) vs 67 (63 to 72); PSA level before ADT – median (IQR) 56 (19 to 165) vs 51 (19 to 158)" (Baseline characteristics)
1.3.justification: The baseline characteristics are balanced and any minor differences are compatible with chance, indicating no problem with the randomisation process.

2.1.quote: "We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy)." (Methods)
2.1.justification: The trial description provides no indication of blinding or use of matched placebos, implying participants knew their treatment allocation.

2.2.quote: "We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy)." (Methods)
2.2.justification: Because the intervention involved distinct medication regimens without blinding procedures, healthcare providers delivering the treatments were aware of the assigned groups.

3.1.quote: "A total of 1917 patients underwent randomization... The median follow‑up was 40 months. There were 184 deaths in the combination group and 262 in the ADT‑alone group." (Results)
3.1.justification: All randomized participants were accounted for either as deaths or censored at the last known follow‑up, indicating outcome data were available for virtually all.

4.1.quote: "The primary outcome measure was overall survival, defined as the time from randomization to death from any cause." (Methods)
4.1.justification: Overall survival is an objective, universally accepted endpoint with no validity concerns.

4.2.quote: "The primary outcome was overall survival." (Methods) and no indication of differing follow‑up procedures between arms.
4.2.justification: Death ascertainment was performed identically for both groups.

4.3.justification: Not applicable because measurement was objective and identical across groups.

4.4.justification: Per Sterne et al. 2019: knowledge is unlikely to influence vital-status or biomarker outcomes without judgment (e.g., all-cause mortality).

5.1.quote: "Registered primary endpoint: Overall survival (time from randomization to death from any cause)" (Registration)
5.1.justification: The primary outcome (overall survival) was pre‑specified in the trial registration and the analysis follows the described Cox model with stratification factors.

5.2.quote: "hazard ratio for death, 0.63; 95% confidence interval [0.52 to 0.76]" (Results Section)
5.2.justification: Only a single pre‑specified measurement (hazard ratio for overall survival) is presented; no alternative scales or time points were selectively reported.

5.3.quote: "Standard survival‑analysis methods were used… Cox proportional‑hazards models to estimate relative treatment effects… adjusted for stratification factors" (Statistical Analysis Plan)
5.3.justification: The analysis conforms to the pre‑specified statistical plan; no evidence of selective reporting of alternative analyses.

2.3.justification: No trial-context-driven deviations such as cross‑overs or contamination are reported.

2.6.quote: "All the patients were included in the efficacy analyses under their assigned treatment on an intention-to-treat basis." (Statistical Analysis)
2.6.justification: The primary analysis of overall survival was performed using all randomized participants according to their assigned groups, which meets the criteria for an appropriate ITT analysis.

domain_rationales.D4: 4.1 and 4.2 are N/PN and 4.3 or 4.4 is N/PN -> Low
domain_rationales.D2: Part1=Low (Part1 Low condition met); Part2=Low (2.6=Y/PY (appropriate ITT analysis) -> Part2=Low)
